verdict.answer_first: A opens with the direct definition and purpose: "a separate data structure...to speed up lookups"; B does too, but A is tighter.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a clear rule: "index the columns you frequently search, join, or sort on in large tables; skip..." while B adds more side cases.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it gives the measure-add-confirm loop: "Run your slow query through EXPLAIN ANALYZE...Add the index, re-run" without extra distractions.
